originalTitle: TSMC struggles to keep up with AI demand: ‘We can only support so much’
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. - the world's biggest semiconductor-maker - is struggling to meet demands from American customers even with its factory buildout in the US, according to reports from Reuters and Bloomberg. "Customer demand is so high, and we can only support so much," TSMC CEO C.C. Wei said after a shareholder meeting on […]
content: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. — the world’s biggest semiconductor-maker — is struggling to meet demands from American customers even with its factory buildout in the US, according to reports from Reuters and Bloomberg. “Customer demand is so high, and we can only support so much,” TSMC CEO C.C. Wei said after a shareholder meeting on Thursday, Reuters reports. “We are doing our best to ensure TSMC does not become a bottleneck.” The surge in AI use has already put constraints on the memory industry, with the widespread shortage of RAM and NAND Flash memory expected to last for years. The AI boom is boosting the sale of semiconductors as well, which could become a $1 trillion industry by 2027, according to research from Deloitte. While Wei said he’d “like” to raise TSMC’s prices, the company wouldn’t issue an abrupt increase like we’ve seen with DRAM and SSDs, as reported by Reuters. Wei added it could take a “very long time” to fulfill its customers’ needs with US-based production, according to Reuters. TSMC has already opened a factory in Arizona and plans to invest $165 billion to build three additional plants in the US, alongside two advanced packaging facilities and a research and development center.
chineseTitle: 台积电难以满足 AI 需求：“我们只能支持这么多”
chineseSummary: 据路透社和彭博社报道，全球最大的半导体制造商台积电尽管在美国扩建工厂，但仍难以满足美国客户的需求。台积电首席执行官 C.C. 表示：“客户的需求如此之高，我们只能支持这么多。”魏在股东大会后表示[...]
chineseContent: 据路透社和彭博社报道，全球最大的半导体制造商台积电尽管在美国扩建工厂，但仍难以满足美国客户的需求。台积电首席执行官 C.C. 表示：“客户的需求如此之高，我们只能支持这么多。”据路透社报道，魏在周四的股东大会后表示。 “我们正在尽最大努力确保台积电不会成为瓶颈。”人工智能使用的激增已经给内存行业带来了限制，RAM 和 NAND 闪存的普遍短缺预计将持续数年。人工智能的繁荣也促进了半导体的销售，根据德勤的研究，到 2027 年，半导体行业可能会成为一个价值 1 万亿美元的行业。虽然魏表示他“希望”提高台积电的价格，但该公司不会像路透社报道的那样突然提高 DRAM 和 SSD 的价格。据路透社报道，魏补充说，美国生产可能需要“很长时间”才能满足客户的需求。台积电已经在亚利桑那州开设了一家工厂，并计划投资 1650 亿美元在美国再建三座工厂，以及两座先进的封装设施和一个研发中心。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
